enrichment.short_description: Cloud case management platform built specifically for personal injury law firms, combining intake, matters, and built-in AI.
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, NY, United States
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CloudLex is cloud case management software for personal injury firms, with intake, document, settlement tools and Lexee AI.

features.title: Intake management
features.description: Shareable intake forms and lead capture with one-click conversion of leads into matters.

features.title: Matter management
features.description: Centralizes case tasks, deadlines, documents, and contacts in a single record per case.

features.title: Document management
features.description: Organizes and shares case documents with AI-driven search across files.

features.title: Calendar and deadline tracking
features.description: Shared visibility into court dates and statute-of-limitations deadlines across the firm.

features.title: Medical records tracking
features.description: Requests and tracks medical records as part of the case file.

features.title: Settlement management
features.description: Tracks demands, offers, and fee calculations through case resolution.

features.title: Lexee AI
features.description: Built-in AI for demand drafting, medical record summaries, intake chatbots, and case Q&A.

features.title: Paralegal services
features.description: Add-on services including records retrieval, chronologies, document indexing, and a 24/7 bilingual call center.

pros: Purpose-built for personal injury practice rather than general legal work
Built-in Lexee AI handles demand drafting and medical summaries
Optional paralegal services add records retrieval and a 24/7 bilingual call center
Integrates with Microsoft 365, Gmail, Google Calendar, DocuSign, and Adobe Sign
Hosted on Microsoft Azure with encryption and multi-factor authentication
cons: Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Focused on personal injury, so less suited to firms handling other practice areas
No free trial or free plan information available on the website

faqs.question: What is CloudLex used for?
faqs.answer: CloudLex is case management software focused specifically on personal injury law firms, covering intake through settlement.

faqs.question: Does CloudLex include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Lexee AI component handles demand drafting, medical record summaries, intake chatbots, and case Q&A.

faqs.question: What does CloudLex integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with Microsoft Office 365, Gmail, Google Calendar, DocuSign, and Adobe Sign.

faqs.question: Is CloudLex pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is not listed on the CloudLex website; firms need to contact the company directly.

faqs.question: Where is CloudLex based?
faqs.answer: CloudLex's offices are located in New York City.

use_cases.title: Solo personal injury attorney
use_cases.description: Manage intake, deadlines, and settlements in one platform without separate case management tools.

use_cases.title: Growing PI firm
use_cases.description: Centralize case documents, medical records, and settlement tracking as caseload and staff grow.
use_cases.audience: Small to mid-size personal injury firms

use_cases.title: High-volume mass tort practice
use_cases.description: Use Lexee AI and paralegal services to handle document-heavy caseloads such as mass tort and product liability.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CloudLex?
paragraphs: CloudLex is case management software built specifically for personal injury law firms, unifying case operations from intake through settlement.

The platform combines case management tools, a built-in AI assistant called Lexee AI, and optional paralegal services within a single system, and supports practice areas such as motor vehicle accidents, slip-and-fall, product liability, mass tort, workers' compensation, and wrongful death.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who CloudLex is for
paragraphs: CloudLex is aimed at personal injury law firms of varying sizes, from solo practitioners to larger firms managing high case volumes, rather than general-purpose legal practices.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-native eDiscovery platform with AI-powered document review, priced by data volume with unlimited users.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Everlaw is an AI-powered eDiscovery platform for litigation and investigations, priced by data usage with unlimited users.

features.title: Deep Dive
features.description: Analyzes large document sets to surface insights with direct citations, used on datasets of 10+ million documents.

features.title: Coding Suggestions
features.description: Automates first-pass document review with AI-driven categorization.

features.title: Writing Assistant
features.description: Helps synthesize evidence and brainstorm case strategy from reviewed material.

features.title: Predictive Coding and Clustering
features.description: AI tools that group and prioritize documents for review.

features.title: Storybuilder
features.description: Trial preparation tool for organizing evidence into a narrative.

features.title: Communication Visualizer
features.description: Provides analytics and visual insight into communication data during eDiscovery.

pros: Usage-based pricing with no per-user fees or upload limits
Single-document AI actions and the Writing Assistant included in subscriptions at no extra cost
Used by law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies
Deep Dive analysis has been used on datasets exceeding 10 million documents
cons: No public pricing; quotes require scheduling a meeting or a phone call
Batch AI actions require separately purchased credits, and unused committed credits can expire
No free trial or free plan mentioned on the site

faqs.question: How is Everlaw priced?
faqs.answer: Everlaw uses usage-based pricing tied to the amount of data managed, with no limits on users or uploads, offered as case-based or annual subscriptions.

faqs.question: Does Everlaw charge per user?
faqs.answer: No, Everlaw states there are no per-user fees and unlimited user licenses are included.

faqs.question: Are AI features included in the subscription?
faqs.answer: Single-document AI actions and the Writing Assistant are included at no extra cost; batch AI actions require separately purchased credits.

faqs.question: Who uses Everlaw?
faqs.answer: Law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies use Everlaw for litigation, investigations, and FOIA requests.

use_cases.title: Large-scale litigation discovery
use_cases.description: Use Deep Dive and predictive coding to analyze millions of documents during litigation.

use_cases.title: Government investigations
use_cases.description: Manage FOIA requests and internal investigations with unlimited users under one subscription.

use_cases.title: Corporate legal early case assessment
use_cases.description: Reduce the volume of documents that reach active review before committing to full discovery.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Everlaw?
paragraphs: Everlaw is a cloud-native eDiscovery platform used by legal professionals to manage litigation and investigations, with AI tools including Deep Dive document analysis, Coding Suggestions, and a Writing Assistant.
Pricing is usage-based, tied to the volume of data managed rather than the number of users, and is sold as case-based or annual subscriptions with quotes provided directly by Everlaw.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Everlaw is for
paragraphs: Everlaw serves law firms, corporate legal departments, and state, local, and federal government agencies handling ediscovery, legal holds, trial preparation, and internal investigations.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered legal data platform used by law firms, enterprises, and government for eDiscovery and investigations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RelativityOne is an AI-powered legal data platform for eDiscovery, investigations, and contract review at enterprise scale.

features.title: aiR Assist
features.description: Conversational AI built into the platform for working with legal data.

features.title: aiR for Review
features.description: Generative AI tool aimed at accelerating document review decisions.

features.title: aiR for Privilege
features.description: AI-assisted workflow for identifying privileged documents.

features.title: aiR for Case Strategy
features.description: AI tool aimed at supporting case strategy development.

features.title: aiR for Data Breach Response
features.description: AI-assisted workflow for responding to data breach investigations.

features.title: Third-party AI integrations
features.description: Integrates with external AI tools including Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

features.title: Security monitoring
features.description: Continuous monitoring and threat detection built into the platform.

pros: Broad generative AI suite (Relativity aiR) covering review, privilege, strategy, and breach response
Integrates with major third-party AI tools including Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini
Used by Fortune 500 companies, AmLaw 200 firms, and government agencies
Company states it has been operating for 25 years
cons: No public pricing; requires visiting a dedicated pricing page or contacting sales
Geared toward large-scale and enterprise use cases rather than solo practitioners
Headquarters and company details beyond location are not detailed on the homepage

faqs.question: What is RelativityOne used for?
faqs.answer: RelativityOne is a legal data platform used for eDiscovery, legal holds, investigations, contract review, and FOIA requests.

faqs.question: Does RelativityOne include AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Relativity aiR suite includes aiR Assist, aiR for Review, aiR for Privilege, aiR for Case Strategy, and aiR for Data Breach Response.

faqs.question: Who uses RelativityOne?
faqs.answer: Law firms, AmLaw 200 firms, Fortune 500 corporations, cyber insurers, academic institutions, and government agencies.

faqs.question: Where is RelativityOne headquartered?
faqs.answer: The company's address listed is 231 South LaSalle Street, Chicago, Illinois.

use_cases.title: Enterprise eDiscovery
use_cases.description: Manage large-scale document review and productions using Relativity aiR tools.
use_cases.audience: AmLaw 200 firms and enterprise legal teams

use_cases.title: Data breach response
use_cases.description: Use aiR for Data Breach Response to help manage investigation workflows after a breach.
use_cases.audience: Corporate legal and cyber insurance teams

use_cases.title: Government FOIA processing
use_cases.description: Handle FOIA and public records requests using the platform's review tools.
use_cases.audience: Federal, state, and local government agencies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RelativityOne?
paragraphs: RelativityOne is an AI-powered legal data platform used for eDiscovery, investigations, and contract review, built around a generative AI suite called Relativity aiR.
The platform integrates with third-party AI tools including Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini, and the company states it has been operating for 25 years.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RelativityOne is for
paragraphs: RelativityOne serves law firms and litigators, AmLaw 200 firms, Fortune 500 corporations, government agencies, cyber insurers, and academic institutions handling complex legal data.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based eDiscovery platform for legal holds, FOIA requests, and document review with AI-powered search.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Logikcull is cloud eDiscovery software with automated processing, AI search, and per-GB or annual subscription pricing.

features.title: Rapid deployment
features.description: Start a new matter in under 30 seconds without IT support.

features.title: Automated processing
features.description: Runs 300+ processing steps including deduplication, OCR, email threading, and metadata extraction.

features.title: Logikcull ASK
features.description: Generative AI search feature that synthesizes facts and timelines from case data.

features.title: PII detection
features.description: Automatically identifies SSNs, addresses, and phone numbers with claimed 99%+ accuracy.

features.title: Bulk redactions
features.description: Template-based redaction system for streamlined document production.

features.title: Direct integrations
features.description: Connects to Google Vault, Microsoft 365, Slack, Box, and Dropbox.

features.title: Legal hold management
features.description: Centralized tracking, reminders, and audit trails for legal holds.

pros: Matters can be started in under 30 seconds without IT involvement
Choice of pay-as-you-go per-GB pricing or annual subscription plans
Direct integrations with Google Vault, Microsoft 365, Slack, Box, and Dropbox
Recognized on G2, Capterra, Gartner Peer Insights, and TrustRadius
Pay-as-you-go accounts include a sample matter for testing
cons: No exact dollar pricing published; per-GB and annual rates require contacting sales
Pay-as-you-go plans have a 10 GB minimum
Best suited to organizations with recurring eDiscovery needs rather than one-off use

faqs.question: How is Logikcull priced?
faqs.answer: Logikcull offers pay-as-you-go monthly pricing based on GB of data stored (10 GB minimum) or annual subscription plans; exact dollar amounts require contacting sales.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Pay-as-you-go accounts include a sample matter that can be used to test the platform before committing to a paid matter.

faqs.question: What does Logikcull ASK do?
faqs.answer: It is a generative AI feature that synthesizes facts and timelines from uploaded case data.

faqs.question: What does Logikcull integrate with?
faqs.answer: It connects directly to Google Vault, Microsoft 365, Slack, Box, and Dropbox.

faqs.question: Who uses Logikcull?
faqs.answer: Corporate legal departments, law firms, government agencies, educational institutions, and internal investigation teams.

use_cases.title: Corporate legal cost reduction
use_cases.description: Bring more eDiscovery work in-house instead of relying entirely on outside counsel.

use_cases.title: Government FOIA and public records
use_cases.description: Process public records and FOIA requests with automated redaction and search.

use_cases.title: Internal HR investigations
use_cases.description: Collect and review internal communications for HR or compliance investigations.

pricing_plans.price: Priced per GB stored, 10 GB minimum

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Logikcull?
paragraphs: Logikcull is a cloud-based discovery automation platform for eDiscovery, legal holds, FOIA requests, and public records management, with automated processing and an AI search feature called Logikcull ASK.
It offers either pay-as-you-go pricing based on data stored per month or an annual subscription plan, with both including unlimited users, projects, and downloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Logikcull is for
paragraphs: Logikcull is used by corporate legal departments, law firms, government agencies, educational institutions, and internal investigation teams handling document collection and review.

enrichment.short_description: AI-driven eDiscovery software and services platform for complex litigation, document review, and legal holds.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: DISCO is an AI-driven eDiscovery platform with Cecilia AI, Auto Review, and legal hold tools for complex litigation.

features.title: Cecilia AI
features.description: Generative AI legal intelligence tool built into the platform.

features.title: Auto Review
features.description: AI-powered document review claimed to process 32,000 documents per hour.

features.title: Legal hold management
features.description: Tools for issuing and tracking legal holds.

features.title: Case Builder
features.description: Deposition management functionality.

features.title: Timelines
features.description: Tool for creating case timelines from reviewed evidence.

features.title: Data request management
features.description: Tools for managing incoming data and discovery requests.

features.title: Professional services
features.description: Includes forensic collections, managed review, and enterprise solutions.

pros: Recognized by G2 as an eDiscovery Leader
Combines software with professional services such as forensic collections and managed review
Cecilia AI and Auto Review aim to speed up document review
Received awards for fastest implementation and estimated ROI
Used by Am Law 200 firms and corporate legal teams on complex matters
cons: No public pricing; requires requesting a quote or demo
Positioned for complex, high-volume litigation rather than small matters
Company size and founding year are not stated on the site

faqs.question: What is DISCO used for?
faqs.answer: DISCO is an AI-driven eDiscovery platform used for document review, legal holds, deposition management, and data request handling in litigation.

faqs.question: What is Cecilia AI?
faqs.answer: Cecilia AI is DISCO's built-in generative AI tool for legal intelligence tasks.

faqs.question: Who uses DISCO?
faqs.answer: Law firms including Am Law 200 firms, corporate legal teams, IP litigation practitioners, and plaintiffs in mass tort and class action litigation.

faqs.question: Where is DISCO headquartered?
faqs.answer: DISCO is headquartered in Austin, Texas.

use_cases.title: Complex litigation document review
use_cases.description: Use Auto Review and Cecilia AI to review large document sets faster.
use_cases.audience: Am Law 200 firms and corporate legal teams

use_cases.title: Mass tort and class action support
use_cases.description: Manage discovery and deposition materials across large plaintiff litigation.
use_cases.audience: Plaintiff's firms in mass tort or class actions

use_cases.title: Legal hold and data request management
use_cases.description: Issue and track legal holds and manage incoming data requests.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is DISCO?
paragraphs: DISCO is an AI-driven eDiscovery software and services platform for litigation support, built around its Cecilia AI generative AI tool and Auto Review document review feature.
The company, headquartered in Austin, Texas, pairs its software with professional services including forensic collections and managed review, and has received G2 recognition as an eDiscovery Leader.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who DISCO is for
paragraphs: DISCO serves law firms including Am Law 200 firms, corporate legal teams, IP litigation practitioners, and plaintiffs in mass tort and class action litigation.

enrichment.short_description: AI-driven eDiscovery platform, founded in 2008, spanning document review, analytics, and legal hold tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Reveal is an AI-driven eDiscovery platform, founded in 2008, offering document review, analytics, and legal hold tools.

features.title: AI-powered document review
features.description: Uses AI to assist with document review and analysis during discovery.

features.title: Advanced analytics
features.description: Data visualization tools for analyzing large discovery datasets.

features.title: Legal hold automation
features.description: Tools for managing and automating legal holds.

features.title: Generative AI models
features.description: Includes reusable generative AI models for discovery workflows.

features.title: Multi-product suite
features.description: Includes Logikcull, Trial Director, Brainspace, Onna, and Reveal Hold alongside the core Reveal platform.

pros: Founded in 2008 with an established multi-product suite
Covers the full discovery lifecycle from collection through trial presentation
Company states $300M in customer cost savings using the platform
Serves corporate legal departments, law firms, ALSPs, government, and education
Multiple industry awards and recognitions cited on the site
cons: No public pricing; requires contacting sales
Headquarters location is not stated on the homepage
The broad multi-product suite may require evaluation to identify the right components

faqs.question: When was Reveal founded?
faqs.answer: Reveal was founded in 2008.

faqs.question: What products does Reveal include?
faqs.answer: The Reveal suite includes the core Reveal platform, Logikcull, Trial Director, Brainspace, Onna, and Reveal Hold.

faqs.question: Who uses Reveal?
faqs.answer: Corporate legal departments, law firms, legal service providers (ALSPs), government agencies, and educational institutions.

faqs.question: Does Reveal include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, the platform includes AI-powered document review, analytics, and reusable generative AI models.

use_cases.title: Multi-matter ALSP operations
use_cases.description: Manage multiple client matters using the combined Reveal product suite.

use_cases.title: Corporate ediscovery consolidation
use_cases.description: Consolidate document review, analytics, and legal hold tools into one suite.

use_cases.title: Government investigations
use_cases.description: Use AI-powered review and analytics for investigative discovery work.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Reveal?
paragraphs: Reveal is an AI-driven eDiscovery platform founded in 2008, offering document review, analytics, and legal hold tools through a multi-product suite that includes Logikcull, Trial Director, Brainspace, Onna, and Reveal Hold.
The company states its platform has contributed to $300M in customer cost savings and has received multiple industry awards.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Reveal is for
paragraphs: Reveal serves corporate legal departments, law firms, legal service providers, government agencies, and educational institutions handling document review and legal investigations.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based eDiscovery platform with per-user pricing, unlimited data hosting, and deposition management tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nextpoint is cloud eDiscovery software with per-user pricing, unlimited data hosting, and document review tools.

features.title: Unlimited data upload
features.description: No per-document or per-GB processing and hosting fees across plans.

features.title: Document review
features.description: Fast search and bulk actions for reviewing case documents.

features.title: Electronic production
features.description: Secure file sharing for producing documents to opposing counsel or courts.

features.title: Deposition management
features.description: Transcript synchronization with video and exhibits.

features.title: Expert witness discovery
features.description: Tools for organizing expert witness materials.

features.title: Custom data analytics
features.description: Filtering tools for working with large datasets.

features.title: Presentation tools
features.description: Features for preparing materials for trial or hearings.

pros: No case fees, upload fees, or data hosting fees on any plan
Simple per-user pricing model rather than per-document or per-GB charges
Experts and co-counsel can be added to a matter at no extra cost
Clients cited include Morgan & Morgan and McDermott Will & Emery
Praised by Law.com for value relative to cost
cons: No dollar pricing published for any of the three tiers
Advanced analytics and auto-redaction features are limited to higher-tier plans
Requires contacting sales for a quote

faqs.question: How is Nextpoint priced?
faqs.answer: Nextpoint uses simple per-user pricing with no case fees, upload fees, or hosting fees; specific dollar amounts require contacting sales.

faqs.question: What plans does Nextpoint offer?
faqs.answer: Nextpoint offers three tiers: Essential, Advanced, and Apex, each adding more analytics and review features.

faqs.question: Does Nextpoint charge for data storage?
faqs.answer: No, all plans include zero data hosting charges regardless of case size.

faqs.question: Who uses Nextpoint?
faqs.answer: Law firms and legal departments of varying sizes, including firms such as Morgan & Morgan and McDermott Will & Emery.

use_cases.title: Small case management
use_cases.description: Handle smaller matters on the Essential plan without data hosting fees.

use_cases.title: Advanced document review
use_cases.description: Use advanced analytics and review templates on the Advanced plan for larger caseloads.

use_cases.title: Enterprise litigation support
use_cases.description: Use the Apex plan's auto-redaction and dedicated customer success team for high-volume litigation.
use_cases.audience: Large firms and enterprise legal teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nextpoint?
paragraphs: Nextpoint is a cloud-based eDiscovery platform that charges per user rather than per document or by data volume, with unlimited data upload included on every plan.
It offers three tiers — Essential, Advanced, and Apex — that add advanced analytics, auto-redaction, and dedicated support as firms move up in plan level.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nextpoint is for
paragraphs: Nextpoint is used by law firms and legal departments of varying sizes, from solo practitioners to larger firms such as Morgan & Morgan and McDermott Will & Emery.

name: TherapyNotes
skip_reason: The official website (therapynotes.com) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden on every fetch attempt across multiple URL variants (www, non-www, root, /pricing, /features), indicating bot-blocking. Facts could not be verified directly from the source, so this entry was skipped rather than relying on third-party review sites.

enrichment.short_description: Mental health EHR and practice management platform for therapists, with AI-assisted notes and a 21-day free trial.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TheraNest is mental health EHR software with AI progress notes, billing, and scheduling, offering a 21-day free trial.

features.title: AI Session Assistant
features.description: Generates automated progress notes from sessions.

features.title: Automated insurance billing
features.description: Handles insurance claims processing automatically.

features.title: Client portal
features.description: Lets clients complete intake forms and request appointments online.

features.title: Reporting and analytics
features.description: Built-in reports for tracking practice performance.

features.title: Intelligent scheduling
features.description: Scheduling with automated reminders to reduce no-shows.

features.title: Secure messaging
features.description: HIPAA-compliant messaging between practitioners and clients.

features.title: Multi-location management
features.description: Supports organizations operating across multiple practice locations.

pros: Offers a 21-day free trial
AI Session Assistant automates progress note writing
HITRUST certified and HIPAA compliant
Supports multi-location group practices, not just solo clinicians
Backed by parent company Ensora Health
cons: Specific subscription pricing is not published on the product page
Now operates under the Ensora Health umbrella, with its original theranest.com domain redirecting to ensorahealth.com
No stated headquarters or founding year on the current product page

faqs.question: Does TheraNest offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, TheraNest offers a 21-day free trial.

faqs.question: Is TheraNest HIPAA compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, TheraNest is HITRUST certified and HIPAA compliant.

faqs.question: Does TheraNest use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AI Session Assistant automatically generates progress notes from sessions.

faqs.question: Is TheraNest still an independent company?
faqs.answer: TheraNest now operates as a product under parent company Ensora Health; its website redirects to the Ensora Health domain.

faqs.question: Who is TheraNest for?
faqs.answer: Solo mental health practitioners and group practices, including therapists, counselors, psychiatrists, and marriage/family therapists.

use_cases.title: Solo therapist documentation
use_cases.description: Use the AI Session Assistant to speed up progress note writing after each session.

use_cases.title: Group practice billing
use_cases.description: Automate insurance claims processing across multiple clinicians.

use_cases.title: Multi-location clinics
use_cases.description: Manage scheduling and records across several practice locations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TheraNest?
paragraphs: TheraNest is a mental health EHR and practice management platform covering documentation, scheduling, billing, and client management, including an AI Session Assistant that automates progress notes.

The product now operates under parent company Ensora Health, with its original theranest.com domain redirecting to the Ensora Health website, though the TheraNest brand name is retained on the product page.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TheraNest is for
paragraphs: TheraNest is built for solo mental health practitioners and group practices, including therapists, counselors, psychiatrists, and marriage and family therapists.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based clinic management platform for health and wellness practitioners, priced per practitioner from CAD $54/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jane App is clinic management software for health and wellness practitioners, priced per practitioner from CAD $54/month.

features.title: Online booking and scheduling
features.description: Lets clients book appointments online directly into the practice calendar.

features.title: Telehealth
features.description: Secure video consultations built into the platform.

features.title: AI-assisted clinical documentation
features.description: AI Scribe add-on assists with generating clinical notes.

features.title: Intake forms
features.description: Digital intake forms collected before or at the first appointment.

features.title: Billing and insurance processing
features.description: Handles invoicing and insurance billing, available as an add-on.

features.title: Jane Payments
features.description: Built-in payment processing for the clinic.

features.title: Patient mobile app
features.description: Secure messaging and appointment access for patients.

features.title: Automated reminders
features.description: Email and SMS appointment reminders to reduce no-shows.

pros: Transparent, published per-practitioner pricing starting at CAD $54/month
Core practice management features included in every plan
AI Scribe available as an add-on for clinical documentation
Serves multiple disciplines including physiotherapy, massage therapy, counselling, and midwifery
Demo account available through support for exploring the platform
cons: No free trial; only a demo account is offered instead
AI Scribe, group telehealth, insurance billing, and Jane Websites are paid add-ons on top of the base plan
Pricing increases with additional practitioners beyond the plan's included seat

faqs.question: Does Jane App offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No, Jane App does not offer a free trial; instead it provides access to a demo account through its community and support team.

faqs.question: How much does Jane App cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at CAD $54/month for the Balance plan, with Practice at CAD $79/month and Thrive at CAD $99/month.

faqs.question: Is AI Scribe included in the base price?
faqs.answer: No, AI Scribe is a paid add-on at $15/month per opted-in practitioner.

faqs.question: Where is Jane App based?
faqs.answer: Jane App's home base is North Vancouver, Canada, with a remote-first team.

faqs.question: Who is Jane App for?
faqs.answer: Health and wellness practitioners such as physiotherapists, massage therapists, counsellors, and midwives running interdisciplinary clinics.

use_cases.title: Solo practitioner clinic
use_cases.description: Manage bookings, notes, and payments on the entry-level Balance plan.

use_cases.title: Interdisciplinary wellness clinic
use_cases.description: Coordinate scheduling and billing across multiple practitioner types in one clinic.

use_cases.title: Insurance-billing practices
use_cases.description: Add the insurance billing add-on to handle claims alongside client payments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jane App?
paragraphs: Jane App is cloud-based practice management software for health and wellness clinics, covering online booking, telehealth, clinical documentation, billing, and payments in one platform.
Pricing is based on the number of practitioners in a clinic, starting at CAD $54/month, with add-ons available for AI-assisted documentation, group telehealth, insurance billing, and clinic websites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jane App is for
paragraphs: Jane App is built for health and wellness practitioners running interdisciplinary clinics, including physiotherapists, massage therapists, counsellors, and midwives.
